source.notes: Genre-neutral departure/delivery language must not receive Forty Thousand broker/lien/cargo fact locks or transport hard-boundary gates.

frame.title: The Sealed Letter
frame.premise: An envoy carries a sealed letter through a disputed pass.
frame.centralTension: The envoy must decide whom to trust before taking the road.
frame.objective: Choose the route and terms before leaving the court.
frame.crucible: Every patron wants the letter opened for a different reason.

outcomeSpectrum.clean: The envoy leaves with a clear patron and route.
outcomeSpectrum.costly: The envoy leaves owing a favor.
outcomeSpectrum.catastrophic: The letter's secrecy is broken before departure.

pacingContract.chapterQuestion: Who does the envoy trust before taking the road?
acceptableResolutions: The envoy chooses a patron and route.
The envoy refuses the court pressure and leaves alone.
pacingContract.middlePressure: The letter becomes harder to keep sealed.
pacingContract.latePressure: The road decision must be made.
closeWhenAny: The envoy leaves the court with a chosen route.
avoidExtendingFor: More court gossip after the departure decision is made.

turns.playerInput: I read the room before answering.
turns.narratorProse: The court waits for your choice.
The steward marks the northern pass on your map.

turns.playerInput: We depart at dawn to deliver the sealed letter.
turns.narratorProse: Dawn puts pale light on the road outside court.

input.playerInput: We depart at dawn to deliver the sealed letter.

narrator.prose: The road takes the court noise behind you.

stateInput.playerInput: We depart at dawn to deliver the sealed letter.
